UEFA Conference League Magazine
Europa League and Conference League Magazine
Archived UEFA Cup finals
programme.sub-title: FC Liverpool - Deportivo Alaves (2001)
programme.desc: A program that briefly presents the UEFA Cup finals, from the time when the two-legged final was replaced by one match in 1998, until the competition was transformed into the Europa League in the 2008/09 season.During this time, the games slowly changed their form.From the 1999/2000 season, teams that took third place in the Champions League group stage joined the competition.In the 2004/05 season, the cup system was changed to a group-cup system.There were qualifying rounds and the first round in a two-legged format.In the second round, the teams were divided into groups of five, but they played only one match against each other.24 teams advanced to the third round, joined by eight teams from the third places of the Champions League group stage.From the third round, a two-legged system was used until the final, which was played as one match from 1998. The final match of the UEFA Cup in the 2000/2001 season was an incredible spectacle that fans will remember for decades.The revelation of the competition, Deportivo Alaves, faced the favored Liverpool.The Spanish team defeated Kaiserslautern 9-2 in the semi-final.This result was a preview of the great emotions that this team was to provide fans with in the fight against The Reds.It was an extraordinary match, full of twists and spectacular moves.A great football show that was a great answer to the question why so many people around the world love football.

UEFA Europa League Archive
programme.sub-title: Legia Warszawa - Rapid Bukareszt 03.11.2011 - sezon 2011/2012
programme.desc: In the program, viewers will see fragments of memorable matches played in the UEFA Europa League.From the 2009/2010 season, this competition replaced the previous UEFA Cup.The authors pay special attention to matches involving Polish clubs.The first Polish team to win the right to play in the LE group stage was Lech Poznań, later Wisła Kraków and Legia Warszawa also achieved this feat.The greatest success so far was promotion to the 1/16 finals of Lech in the 2010/2011 season, Legia and Wisła in the 2011/2012 season and Legia Warszawa in the 2014/2015 season.

programme.title: Football: UEFA Europa League - match: Aston Villa FC - FC Salzburg
programme.desc: The hosts are impressive in their form both domestically and in European cups.In the current edition of the Europa League, they lost only once - to Go Ahead Eagles (1-2), but since then they have won four in a row, including the last one over Fenerbahce (1-0).Aston Villa is already certain of promotion, taking second place in the table.The situation of the Austrians is definitely more difficult.RB Salzburg managed to win only two games and lost five.They are in a distant 28th place in the classification, and in order to advance to the play-off phase of the 1/16 finals, they must win in Birmingham and count on their rivals' mistakes.

programme.title: Football: Turkish Cup - final match: Trabzonspor - Konyaspor
Football: German Cup - final match: FC Bayern Munich - VfB Stuttgart
programme.desc: Bayern has been undisputedly dominating the Bundesliga for years, but in the five previous editions of the DFB-Pokal it only managed to reach the quarter-finals.Vincent Kompany's players are only a decisive battle away from breaking the spell of this competition and sealing the double crown.The Die Schwaben team returns to the Olympic Stadium in Berlin knowing that a much more difficult task awaits them than last season, when they won the national cup at the expense of Arminia Bielefeld, then in the third league (4:2).

UEFA Europa League Archive
programme.sub-title: Lech Poznań - Juventus Turyn 01.12.2010 - sezon 2010/2011
programme.desc: In the program, viewers will see fragments of memorable matches played in the UEFA Europa League.From the 2009/2010 season, this competition replaced the previous UEFA Cup.The authors pay special attention to matches involving Polish clubs.The first Polish team to win the right to play in the LE group stage was Lech Poznań, later Wisła Kraków and Legia Warszawa also achieved this feat.The greatest success so far was promotion to the 1/16 finals of Lech in the 2010/2011 season, Legia and Wisła in the 2011/2012 season and Legia Warszawa in the 2014/2015 season.

programme.desc: The White and Reds will play the second sparring in Sosnowiec.For the rejuvenated Polish national team, this is an opportunity to prepare for the LN.Nikola Grbic's team lost the previous match against Ukraine in five games.

programme.title: Men's volleyball: Champions League - semi-final match: PGE Projekt Warszawa - Sir Sicoma Monini Perugia
programme.desc: This year, for the first time in history, Projekt from Warsaw was among the best teams on the Old Continent.The path of the capital team to the final tournament of the Champions League was not strewn with roses.Promotion to the play-off phase by a shot, then the golden set against Itas Trentino and the fierce competition against Bogdanka LUK Lublin in the quarterfinals showed that volleyball players from the capital do not give up in the most difficult moments.They will fight for promotion to the grand final against the defender of the trophy, Sir Sicoma Monini Perugia, whose important player is the Polish representative, Kamil Semeniuk.

Tennis: ATP Tournament in Hamburg - summary
programme.desc: The tournament played on the Am Rothenbaum courts was a dress rehearsal for its participants before the French Open.Defending champion Flavio Cobolli said goodbye to the competition in the first round after losing to Ignacio Buse.The Peruvian's triumph in this match was a preview of what he would do in the next stages of the competition.The 22-year-old tennis player eliminated higher-ranked rivals one by one.In the final, he faced sixth-seeded Tommy Paul and emerged victorious.It was the greatest success in the career of the young South American player so far.

programme.desc: Bayern has been undisputedly dominating the Bundesliga for years, but in the five previous editions of the DFB-Pokal it only managed to reach the quarter-finals.Vincent Kompany's players are only a decisive battle away from breaking the spell of this competition and sealing the double crown.The Die Schwaben team returns to the Olympic Stadium in Berlin knowing that a much more difficult task awaits them than last season, when they won the national cup at the expense of Arminia Bielefeld, then in the third league (4:2).
